pi_items.metadata_value: CRA T4 and T4A Slip Formats 2024; CRA Guide T4002 Section 5 (Real Estate Agents)
pi_items.source: Analysis of scenario_espinoza_realestate.txt confirms the data is synthetically generated for a hypothetical taxpayer. The text file internally states its source as 'CRA T4, T4A Slips and Real Estate Business Records'. Furthermore, CRA Guide T4002 Chapter/Section 5 is generally about 'Losses', not specifically 'Real Estate Agents'.
Confirmed via scenario_espinoza_realestate.txt which lists 'Full Name: Rodrigo Espinoza'.
Confirmed via scenario_espinoza_realestate.txt which lists 'Province: Alberta (AB)'.

pi_items.metadata_value: Real estate agent (Century 21) — dual status employee/contractor

pi_items.metadata_value: Split T4 salary + T4A commission; T2125 with marketing/professional/vehicle/home-office expenses; vehicle CCA class 10; charitable donation; common-law partner
pi_items.verified_value: Split T4 salary + T4A commission; T2125 with marketing/professional/vehicle/home-office expenses; vehicle CCA class 10; charitable donation; common-law partner
pi_items.source: Confirmed via scenario_espinoza_realestate.txt which contains a $36,000 T4 salary, $87,500 T4A commission, various T2125 expenses, a Class 10 vehicle CCA, a $500 charitable donation, and a common-law partner (Isabella Morales).
Confirmed via task.json description, setup_task.ps1 script, and verifier.py logic.
Confirmed via setup_task.ps1 which copies 'scenario_espinoza_realestate.txt' into the environment.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires completing a 2024 Canadian tax return for a synthetic taxpayer named Rodrigo Espinoza in Alberta (AB). The scenario involves a dual status real estate agent (Century 21) with a split T4 base salary ($36,000) and T4A commission income ($87,500). The return requires filing a T2125 with various business expenses including marketing, professional fees, a Class 10 vehicle CCA calculation, and home office expenses. Additionally, the return must include a $500 charitable donation and a common-law partner (Isabella Morales). The final StudioTax file must be saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\StudioTax\rodrigo_espinoza.24t.
